title: portulaca quadrifida
intro: Portulaca quadrifida, also known as Indian starflower or rosary pea, is a flowering plant species native to tropical Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial compounds, which have been used in traditional medicine for centuries to treat various ailments. Portulaca quadrifida is believed to have several health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and acting as an antioxidant, although more research is needed to confirm these effects. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in infusions, decoctions, or as a dried powder, and it is also used in Ayurvedic and Unani medicine.
